answer.text: The minimum percentage of deposits banks must lend to priority sectors
The minimum percentage of deposits banks must hold as cash reserves with the central bank
The interest rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks
The rate at which banks lend to each other overnight

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CRR is the minimum percentage of total deposits that commercial banks must hold as cash reserves with the central bank (RBI). **The Trap:** Priority sector lending is a separate requirement. Lending rate is repo rate. Interbank lending rate is call money rate. **Cross-Exam Context:** RBI policy tools tested in NISM Series XV, IV, and I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** CRR changes have immediate liquidity impact. A 0.25% CRR increase on Rs.200 lakh crore deposits drains Rs.50,000 crore from the banking system.
